elements.text: Underwriting Standards
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Every loan sold to Fannie Mae must be underwritten in order to establish that the borrower has the ability, willingness, and capacity to repay the debt. Sellers must have adequate internal controls and processes in place to evaluate borrower income and liabilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements in the following table apply to all loans sold to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The seller must...
elements.label: text

elements.text: analyze a borrower's repayment capacity, including an evaluation of the borrower's capacity to
elements.label: text

elements.text: repay the debt by its final maturity, assuming a fully amortizing repayment schedule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain written policies and procedures for how it takes into account income or assets, debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations, alimony, child support, and monthly DTI ratio in its ability to repay determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The seller must...
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: if risk-layering is involved:
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrate the existence of effective compensating factors that support the seller's underwriting decision and borrower's repayment capacity,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: have clear policies governing the use of risk-layering features,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: not rely solely on one factor to compensate for the risk, but instead must consider a combination of compensating factors.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: verify and document the borrower's income (both source and amount), assets, and liabilities used in the underwriting decision, except as otherwise expressly provided in the Selling Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: analyze the borrower's DTI, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: total monthly housing-related payments, (principal, interest, taxes, insurance, and other property-related assessments); and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: long-term and significant short-term monthly debts
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: ensure the final loan application signed by the borrower at closing includes all income and debts of the borrower that were verified, disclosed, or identified during the mortgage process and considered by the lender in the qualification for the loan, except as otherwise expressly provided in the Selling Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender may be required to repurchase a loan that is in breach of the requirements of this topic at any time notwithstanding that the loan is otherwise eligible for relief from enforcement for breaches of certain underwriting and eligibility representations and warranties in accordance with A2-3.2-02, Enforcement Relief for Breaches of Certain Representations and Warranties Related to Underwriting and Eligibility

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Arrangements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter explains Fannie Mae's requirements regarding the outsourcing of mortgage origination and servicing functions. It also addresses other servicing arrangements as well as the requirements related to document custody and document custodians.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A3-3-01, Outsourcing of Mortgage Processing and Third-Party Originations (02/07/2024)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on the outsourcing of mortgage originations to third parties, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Third-Party Originations
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Management Procedures for Third-Party Originations
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Third-Party Originations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A third-party origination is any loan that is completely or partially originated, processed, underwritten, packaged, funded, or closed by an entity other than the seller (or its parent company, controlled affiliate or subsidiary) that sells the loan to Fannie Mae. This includes mortgage brokers or correspondents, which are known as third-party originators. If a seller enters into a contract with a third party known for the quality of its underwriting (such as a mortgage insurer) to help the seller in underwriting its mortgage originations, the loans will not be considered third-party originations. Refer to Chapter E-3, Glossary for the definition of correspondent and broker. (Retail is also an origination type but is not considered a third-party origination.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers remain fully responsible to Fannie Mae for functions that are outsourced to third parties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certain Special Feature Codes (see Special Feature Codes) are required at delivery for third-party originations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A seller must have effective written policies and procedures for the approval and management of third-party originations and must satisfy itself that the third-party originator is capable of producing quality loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management Procedures for Third-Party Originations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Effective management procedures for third-party originations include the items in the table below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Management Procedures for Third-Party Originations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A system for evaluating and approving third-party originators to ensure that they produce quality loans. The seller's procedures must include a review of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recent financial statements;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: current licenses;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: resumes or other appropriate information of principal officers managing the underwriting and originating process;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the third party's QC procedures so that the seller can determine if the party and its originations meet the seller's standards for quality;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: results of background checks for principal officers; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the third-party originator's hiring procedure for checking all employees involved in the origination of loans (including application through closing) against:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: ⚬ the U.S. General Services Administration Excluded Parties List,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: ⚬ the HUD Limited Denial of Participation List, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: ⚬ the Federal Housing Finance Agency Suspended Counterparty Program List.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: A method for verifying, and periodically reverifying, a third-party originator's compliance with applicable laws, licensing, and qualifications for originating loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A method for confirming that a third-party originator meets its contract with the seller and the seller's Lender Contract with Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A requirement that a third-party originator have a written QC plan and a method to validate the existence of that plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A process for resolving QC discrepancies and tracking corrective actions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A requirement for submitting periodic reports on activity and performance issues to the seller's senior management.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standards for evaluating a third-party originator's performance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Provisions for suspending or terminating the third-party originator's relationship.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Annual review of the third-party originator's financial statements to determine that it is financially viable and capable of meeting its obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Quarterly review of the performance of loans originated by the third-party originator.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026

elements.text: A3-3-02, Concurrent Servicing Transfers (05/01/2024)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on concurrent servicing transfers, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Concurrent Servicing Transfers
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Servicer Eligibility Criteria
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Servicing Assignment Contract
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Notification of Concurrent Servicing Transfers
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Termination of Concurrent Servicing Transfers
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Servicer Eligibility Criteria for Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Seller Eligibility Criteria for Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Servicing Marketplace (SMP) Bifurcation Terms and Conditions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Concurrent Servicing Transfers
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A concurrent servicing transfer (also known as a transfer of servicing concurrent with delivery) occurs when a selling lender transfers the servicing rights for a loan to a Fannie Mae-approved servicer at the same time it sells the loan to Fannie Mae. This is an 'automatic' transfer because Fannie Mae's prior approval of the transaction is not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the selling lender is servicing the loans prior to delivery and will not be servicing the loans after delivery, the selling lender may automatically transfer servicing to a lender that is eligible to service them for Fannie Mae, and has agreed to do so, effective concurrently with delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae. The lender must notify Fannie Mae at the time of loan delivery that servicing has been transferred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the selling lender may designate the servicing lender as Fannie Mae's servicer for the loans by notifying Fannie Mae at the time of delivery if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the selling lender is not servicing the loans prior to delivery because it has contracted with another lender (the 'servicing lender') to service the loans for the selling lender;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: the selling lender will not be servicing the loans after delivery;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the servicing lender is eligible to service the loans for Fannie Mae; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the servicing lender agrees to service the loans for Fannie Mae, which requires the contractual servicing relationship be with Fannie Mae instead of with the seller.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the servicing lender wants the contractual servicing relationship to be with the selling lender instead of with Fannie Mae, even after delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae, the selling lender must become Fannie Mae's servicer (as 'master servicer'), and the servicing lender must become a 'subservicer.' (See A3-3-03, Other Servicing Arrangements, and the Servicing Guide.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A transfer of servicing that becomes effective concurrent with delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae must be implemented in accordance with the requirements in the Servicing Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: After Fannie Mae has purchased or securitized a loan, Fannie Mae must approve all subsequent assignments of servicing related to that loan before the servicing can be transferred. See the Servicing Guide for additional requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Eligibility Criteria
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The transferee servicer must meet Fannie Mae's eligibility criteria that apply to a lender that becomes Fannie Mae's servicer in a post-delivery transfer of servicing as described in the Servicing Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Assignment Contract
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The servicing transfer agreement between the lender and the transferee servicer must provide (among other requirements) that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the effective date for transfer of the servicing of the loans will be no later than the date Fannie Mae funds the whole loan delivery or issues the MBS;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae may request and obtain (at any time) a copy of such agreement; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the agreement must provide, for the stated benefit of Fannie Mae, that the transferee servicer, as of the effective date:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: accepts the servicing portfolio and agrees to service the loans in accordance with all Fannie Mae requirements;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: assumes responsibility for all of the lender's contractual obligations related to all servicing duties, obligations and responsibilities related to the loans that arose prior to the delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: has performed due diligence review(s) of the servicing portfolio to its satisfaction, which includes examination of the books, records, and custodial accounts of the lender with respect to the servicing portfolio;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: assumes full responsibility to Fannie Mae for the correctness of such books and records; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: represents and warrants that the provisions of any agreement between the servicer and any other party providing for servicing the loans will not continue after the date on which Fannie Mae funds
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: the whole loan delivery or issues the MBS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: By accepting a transfer of servicing, the transferee servicer agrees to the above matters and represents and warrants that they are correct (as applicable), even in those cases in which the contractual relationship between the lender and the transferee servicer is such that no agreement to assign the servicing is legally necessary at the time the loans are delivered to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Further, by designating another lender as servicer of the loans on the applicable loan schedule, the lender represents and warrants that with respect to such loans:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the servicer has agreed to the above matters and represents and warrants that they are correct (as applicable), and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the provisions of any agreement between the lender and any other party providing for servicing of the loans will not continue after the date on which Fannie Mae funds the whole loan delivery or issues the MBS.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: However, the lender is not released from any liabilities to Fannie Mae with respect to the loans or the servicing of them prior to the delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae. The lender and the servicer will be jointly and severally liable to Fannie Mae for the obligations and liabilities related to the loans or the servicing of them that arise before delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements of this section, a transfer of servicing that becomes effective concurrent with delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae must be implemented in accordance with Fannie Mae's requirements in the Servicing Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: After Fannie Mae has purchased or securitized a loan, Fannie Mae must approve all subsequent assignments of servicing related to that loan before the servicing can be transferred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notification of Concurrent Servicing Transfers
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender must notify Fannie Mae of the transferee servicer by entering the transferee servicer's nine-digit Fannie Mae seller/servicer number into the Loan Delivery application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Termination of Concurrent Servicing Transfers
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If a concurrent servicing transfer does not meet Fannie Mae's eligibility standards as stated in this Guide and in the Servicing Guide , Fannie Mae is entitled to terminate the transferee's servicing with respect to the affected loans in order to transfer servicing of the loans to another servicer, pursuant to Fannie Mae's rights under the MSSC. The lender is obligated for all losses incurred by Fannie Mae resulting from the lender's designation of an ineligible servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information about concurrent servicing transfers, see the Servicing Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Servicing Marketplace is a committing platform that provides a standardized process for sellers and servicers who engage in concurrent servicing transfer transactions. Seller and servicer obligations for loans sold and servicing acquired via the Servicing Marketplace will be bifurcated under the terms and conditions below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Servicer Eligibility Criteria for Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The transferee servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet Fannie Mae's eligibility criteria that apply to a lender that becomes Fannie Mae's servicer in a postdelivery transfer of servicing as described in the Servicing Guide, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: obtain Fannie Mae's approval to participate in Servicing Marketplace.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: See  A2-1-01, Contractual Obligations for Sellers/Servicers  for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller Eligibility Criteria for Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: In order to be eligible as a seller for Servicing Marketplace, at a minimum, sellers must meet and maintain an Adjusted Net Worth, calculated in accordance with  A4-1-01, Maintaining Seller/Servicer Eligibility, of at least $2.5 million, plus an amount equal to the greater of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sum of the following:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 0.25% of the Residential First Lien Mortgage Servicing UPB serviced for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, plus
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 0.35% of the Residential First Lien Mortgage Servicing UPB serviced for Ginnie Mae, plus
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 0.25% of the Other Servicing UPB, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: 0.25% of the UPB of whole loans purchased by Fannie Mae that were committed in the preceding 36 months via Servicing Marketplace.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The minimum Adjusted Net Worth does not include loans serviced under a subservicing arrangement - that is, for which the seller/servicer is contractually obligated to service for another servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If these minimum requirements are met, Fannie Mae may, but is not required to, approve and activate the seller for Servicing Marketplace. A seller may view whether it has seller access to Servicing Marketplace by logging into the Fannie Mae whole loan committing application. Fannie Mae, in its discretion at any time, may terminate a seller's participation in Servicing Marketplace, which will be effective immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Marketplace (SMP) Bifurcation Terms and Conditions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Using SMP in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application, lenders may arrange for a concurrent sale of servicing to an approved Fannie Mae servicer. See C2-1.3-01, Servicing Marketplace, for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender approved as a seller for SMP transactions is directly liable to Fannie Mae for the obligations and liabilities related to the SMP loans, including all selling representations and warranties required to be made by a seller, and for obligations and liabilities related to servicing of the SMP loans that arise before delivery of the loans to Fannie Mae. The servicer retained by the lender concurrent with Fannie Mae's acquisition of the loan (and any transferee servicer to whom servicing rights and obligations with respect to an SMP loan have been transferred by the servicer with approval from Fannie Mae) is responsible for the servicing duties, obligations and responsibilities related to the loan that arise both prior to Fannie Mae's acquisition of the loan and thereafter, but otherwise the servicer (and any transferee servicer as previously described in this paragraph) has no liability and is not responsible for breaches of any of the selling lender's selling representations, warranties, obligations or liabilities related to the SMP loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The lender's sale of SMP loans to Fannie Mae is subject to the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon the sale of a SMP loan to Fannie Mae, the lender makes all representations and warranties required to be made by a seller under this Selling Guide.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender acknowledges and agrees that:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae is entitled to enforce directly against the lender, and the lender is liable for, any and all remedies (including, without limitation, repurchase) for a breach of the lender's obligations and liabilities related to the SMP loans. Fannie Mae is under no obligation to enforce or attempt to enforce any such remedies against the servicer.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Without limiting the provisions of the Guides, the lender must resolve repurchase requests with respect to SMP loans within the time and manner required by the Servicing Guide . Failure to do so may result in termination of the lender's approval to participate in SMP, as well as any other remedies Fannie Mae may elect to pursue.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the Lender Contract or any other agreement between the lender and Fannie Mae, by its sale of SMP loans to Fannie Mae, the lender:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: authorizes Fannie Mae to disclose from time to time to the applicable servicer any information (and any related assessments and analyses developed by Fannie Mae) that Fannie Mae may have concerning the lender (including, for example, information related to the lender's financial condition and relationship with Fannie Mae), the SMP loans, the servicing of SMP loans and related quality control reports, and waives any requirement that Fannie Mae maintain the confidentiality of such information to the extent such requirement would otherwise prohibit such disclosure; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: shall have no rights or claims against Fannie Mae in connection with such disclosure or transfer of information, and waives any and all claims arising out of or based upon the confidential nature of such information.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender acknowledges that Fannie Mae may, at its sole discretion:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: from time to time, amend or supplement Fannie Mae's procedures and requirements for the purchase of SMP loans, by publishing such amendments or supplementary material in the Selling Guide or in other written communications; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: terminate the lender's approval to participate in SMP by delivering notice to the lender at any time.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The servicer of SMP loans sold to Fannie Mae is subject to the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The servicer must service all SMP loans in accordance with the requirements of the Servicing Guide .
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The servicer waives all rights of recourse against Fannie Mae regarding any sale, transfer or acceptance of SMP loan servicing.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the servicer desires to transfer servicing of any SMP loan, it must comply with the terms of the Servicing Guide and clearly identify all such loans in its request for Fannie Mae's approval of a transfer of servicing.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae is authorized to disclose from time to time to the applicable seller any information provided by the servicer concerning SMP loans, including any SMP loan servicing and quality control reports. The servicer waives any requirement that Fannie Mae maintain the confidentiality of such information to the extent such requirement would otherwise prohibit such disclosure. In addition, the servicer agrees that it
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: has no rights or claims against Fannie Mae in connection with such disclosure or transfer of information and waives all claims arising out of or based upon the confidential nature of any such information.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The servicer may be asked to provide to the applicable seller certain performance data relating to SMP loans which have experienced delinquency and for which there is an increased risk that Fannie Mae or a third party may seek repurchase or other remedies (At Risk loans).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The servicer agrees that, in connection with any disclosure of performance data for At Risk loans it elects to provide to a seller:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The servicer is solely responsible for performing (and has performed) its own analysis of, and determination that any disclosures of performance data are in compliance with the Gramm-LeachBliley Act privacy rules and other applicable legal requirements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: In making disclosures of performance data, the servicer has not relied upon any representations or legal advice of Fannie Mae, whether oral or written, regarding compliance with privacy laws.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The servicer may limit the seller's use of such data to improve its origination processes to prevent future fraud, risk control/estimation of potential repurchase, make whole or indemnification obligations in connection with such At Risk loans or such other purpose as the servicer deems appropriate.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae, at its sole discretion, may:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: from time to time, amend or supplement Fannie Mae's procedures and requirements for the purchase of SMP loans, by publishing such amendments or supplementary material in this Guide or in other written communications; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: terminate the servicer's approval to participate in SMP by delivering notice to the servicer at any time. The provisions of the Guides relating to SMP loans (including that the servicer has no liability and is not responsible for breaches of any of the selling lender's selling representations, warranties, obligations or liabilities related to the SMP loans) will survive termination of the servicer's participation in SMP but only as to SMP loans for which the servicer acquired the related loan servicing prior to the termination of its participation in SMP.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: A3-3-03, Other Servicing Arrangements (12/15/2015)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides an overview of other servicing arrangements, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subservicing
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: General Requirements for Subservicing Arrangements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Pledge of Servicing Rights and Transfer of Interest in Servicing Income
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Subservicing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A lender may use other organizations to perform some or all of its servicing functions. Fannie Mae refers to these arrangements as 'subservicing' arrangements, meaning that a servicer (the 'subservicer') other than the contractually responsible servicer (the 'master' servicer) is performing the servicing functions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are not considered to be subservicing arrangements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: when a computer service bureau is used to perform accounting and reporting functions;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: when the originating lender sells and assigns servicing to another lender, unless the originating lender continues to be the contractually responsible servicer.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: General Requirements for Subservicing Arrangements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A servicer may use a subservicer only if it will not interfere with the servicer's ability to meet Fannie Mae's remitting and reporting requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A master servicer may not enter into new subservicing arrangements-or extend existing arrangements to include newly originated mortgages-unless both the master servicer and the subservicer are Fannie Maeapproved servicers in good standing who are able to perform the duties associated with the master servicer/subservicer arrangement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The master servicer must ensure that its written agreement with the subservicer acknowledges Fannie Mae's right to rescind its recognition of the subservicing arrangement if Fannie Mae decides to transfer the master servicer's portfolio for any reason.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The master servicer must confirm its existing subservicing arrangements when it submits the Lender Record Information (Form 582) each year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information concerning subservicer and master servicer duties, responsibilities, and other requirements, see the Servicing Guide on Fannie Mae's website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pledge of Servicing Rights and Transfer of Interest in Servicing Income
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A lender may enter into one of the following transactions, provided that the purpose of the transaction is a purpose permitted by Fannie Mae and the lender obtains Fannie Mae's prior written consent:
elements.label: text

elements.text: a pledge, or grant of a security interest in, the servicing rights to all or part of its Fannie Mae servicing portfolio, including mortgage loans in MBS pools (a 'pledge of servicing');
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a sale, assignment, transfer, pledge, or hypothecation of all or any portion of its compensation in excess of the amount needed to service mortgage loans for Fannie Mae ('excess servicing compensation'); or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a sale, assignment, transfer, pledge or hypothecation of all or any portion of its right to receive reimbursement of servicing advances.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note : A transaction in either of the last two bullets above is referred to as a 'transfer of an interest in servicing income.'
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender may enter into a pledge of servicing or a transfer of an interest in servicing income for the following purposes only:
elements.label: text

elements.text: to fund the acquisition and performance of required servicing activities for additional servicing and/or servicing portfolios;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: to provide collateral for warehouse lines of credit; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: to effect the purchase of all or substantially all of the assets of a mortgage banking company, including a management buyout of its existing company, or a buyout of the controlling ownership interests of existing shareholders.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must request Fannie Mae's prior approval of a specific pledging transaction or transfer of an interest in servicing income at least 30 days prior to the proposed effective date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A pledge of servicing transaction between the lender and the secured creditor must be documented by a security agreement agreed to by the lender and the secured creditor. The lender, the secured creditor, and Fannie Mae must also execute an acknowledgment agreement acceptable to Fannie Mae, which sets forth the rights and responsibilities of the lender, the secured creditor, and Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A transfer of an interest in servicing income transaction between the lender and the purchaser or financier must be documented by a purchase and sale, security or financing agreement in a form agreed to by the lender and the purchaser or financier. The lender, the purchaser or financier, and Fannie Mae must also execute a subordination of interest agreement acceptable to Fannie Mae, which sets forth the rights and responsibilities of the lender, the purchaser or financier, and Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information about the terms and provisions of the security agreement and the acknowledgment agreement, see the Servicing Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: A3-3-04, Document Custodians (05/04/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides general information on document custodians, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: General Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Selection of a Document Custodian
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Documentation of the Relationship
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Additional Seller/Servicer Obligations Related to Document Custodians
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: General Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Certain documents related to Fannie Mae loans must be held by a Fannie Mae-approved document custodian. The document custodian is acting on behalf of the seller/servicer and Fannie Mae when certifying loan documents and data at the time of acquisition by Fannie Mae. The roles and responsibilities of the document custodian are described in the Fannie Mae Requirements for Document Custodians (RDC guide).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selection of a Document Custodian
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For whole loans and MBS loans (excluding eNotes), the seller/servicer must use one of Fannie Mae's active document custodians.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For whole loans or MBS loans delivered as eNotes, Fannie Mae is the document custodian but has designated certain third parties to certify eNotes. The seller/servicer must use an approved third party to certify eNotes to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae may require a seller/servicer or document custodian to transfer documents to a different document custodian, even if the current custodian satisfies Fannie Mae's requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Fannie Mae's Active Document Custodians for a complete list of eligible document custodians.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation of the Relationship
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The seller must add the document custodian to its profile within the Loan Delivery application. For all mortgage loans, the document custodian will certify the loans through the Document Certification application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each custodian arrangement must be evidenced by the execution of a Master Custodial Agreement (Form 2017). Form 2017 covers custodial arrangements for both whole loans and MBS loans. The seller/servicer sends the completed Form 2017 to the document custodian for execution. Upon receipt, the document custodian will send the seller/servicer ancillary documentation for completion. After the documentation and onboarding are complete, Fannie Mae and the document custodian will execute Form 2017. A copy of the fully executed Form 2017 will be returned to the seller/servicer for its records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: A3-3-05, Custody of Mortgage Documents (05/04/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on the custody of mortgage documents, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview of Custodian Documents
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Release of Custodial Documents
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Transfer and Recertification of Custodial Documents
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview of Custodian Documents
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Custodial documents are the legal mortgage documents an approved document custodian takes into physical custody when Fannie Mae purchases or securitizes a loan. The following documents are key custodial documents for whole loans and MBS loans. All other documents may be held in the individual loan file maintained by the seller/servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See E-2-01, Required Custodial Documents for additional documentation requirements.
elements.label: caption

elements.text: Type of Document
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Original note and note addenda
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other documents needed for certification of whole loans or certification for inclusion in an MBS
elements.label: text

elements.text: pool such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: powers of attorney, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: interest rate buydown agreements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Release of Custodial Documents
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The document custodian must not release custodial documents for either whole loans or MBS loans unless it receives a written request (including in electronic form) containing substantially the same information as required by Request for Release/Return of Documents (Form 2009).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer and Recertification of Custodial Documents
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The servicer of any Fannie Mae loan may transfer related custodial documents to any document custodian with which it has executed a Master Custodial Agreement (Form 2017). The servicer must send a request to Fannie Mae's Custodian Oversight team and include the name of the transferor and transferee custodians and the list of loans requested for transfer (see E-1-02, List of Contacts). Fannie Mae will provide approval for the transfer, which the servicer will be responsible for providing to the transferee and transferor document custodians. The servicer must make appropriate arrangements for the safe transfer of the custodial documents to the new custodian's facilities and for the payment of all costs related to the transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Recertification of custodial documents is required when there is a transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents by the existing servicer to a new document custodian, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: servicing (whether or not there is a change in document custodian).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If a transferee servicer does not want to use the same document custodian that has been holding custodial documents for loans included in a servicing transfer, it must inform Fannie Mae of the change in document custodian arrangements when it sends the Request for Approval of Servicing or Subservicing Transfer (Form 629). The transferee servicer should not submit a custodial agreement executed by the new document custodian(s) until after it receives notification that the servicing transfer has been approved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The transferor servicer must provide the existing document custodian and the new document custodian with a data file that provides certain loan-level information for each loan for which custodial documents are being transferred. The servicer must include the documentation in a separate loan file for each loan being transferred. When the servicer sends individual files, it should band (or box) them together with the trial balance to ensure that the new document custodian can associate the documents with what is delivered in each box.

elements.text: A3-4-01, Confidentiality of Information (02/05/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: This topic contains the following:
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: General Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Information Security Program
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan Quality, Loan Performance Data and NPI
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Feedback on New Products, Product Upgrade, or New Service Offering
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Specific Transactions or Dealings
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae Obligations
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Exclusions from Confidential Information
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: General Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A seller/servicer often obtains confidential information about borrowers, security property, Fannie Mae technologies, products or services and Fannie Mae's business when performing underwriting, origination, selling, servicing, or other activities under the Lender Contract or when previewing technologies, products or services ('Confidential Information').
elements.label: text

elements.text: Confidential Information includes nonpublic personal information (NPI) and all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: information relating to
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: technical specifications;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: product development strategy and activity;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: pricing and financial information;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: designs;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: unpublished patent applications;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: inventions;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: improvements;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: writings and other works of authorship;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: trade secrets;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: drawings;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: models;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: software (including source code and object code), algorithms, and flow charts; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: other documentation;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: all other confidential, proprietary, or trade secret information which a reasonable person would recognize as such, or which is specifically designated as confidential; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: any compilation or summary of the foregoing when disclosed by or on behalf of Fannie Mae to the seller/servicer.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The following table describes Fannie Mae's requirements related to Confidential Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The seller/servicer must...
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Take appropriate steps to ensure the security, integrity, and confidentiality of Confidential Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comply with all relevant applicable laws and regulations, including laws protecting borrower privacy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not disclose Confidential Information to third parties, without Fannie Mae's prior written approval, except on a need-to-know basis to the seller/servicer's partners, affiliates, officers, employees, directors, contractors, counsels, agents, or representatives, provided they are subject to confidentiality obligations at least as stringent as those set forth in this topic.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: : These restrictions do not apply to the extent the seller/servicer is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclose the Confidential Information by applicable law, provided that the seller/servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: uses all reasonable efforts to give Fannie Mae notice at least ten business days prior to such disclosure; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: discloses only that portion of the Confidential Information that the seller/servicer's legal counsel determines is legally required to be furnished, and request that the information remain confidential.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The requirement to provide Fannie Mae notice is waived if the seller/servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: is required by law to disclose in confidence Confidential Information in response to requests from a governmental agency, regulator, or self-regulatory authority that has authority to regulate or oversee the seller/servicer's business (including bank examiners, securities examiners, and regulators' inspector general offices); and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: formally requests that the information be treated in confidence and exempt from the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) and other open records laws requests.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Not use Confidential Information in any way that could be viewed as
elements.label: text

elements.text: a conflict of interest
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a breach of confidentiality or privacy, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the gaining of an unfair advantage from the seller/servicer's relationship with Fannie Mae.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Employ methods for securely disposing or destroying Confidential Information. Implement commercially reasonable measures meeting or exceeding industry standards to ensure the security, integrity, and confidentiality of Confidential Information which, at a minimum must meet the requirements outlined in the Supplement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Promptly notify Fannie Mae in accordance with the requirements in the Supplement .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The seller/servicer must have the borrower's authorization to disclose borrower NPI, unless permitted by applicable law, and may disclose information about a borrower's payment history to a third party if the borrower submits written authorization. The information disclosed must be accurate, complete, and easily understandable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae may seek immediate equitable relief to enjoin any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information, in addition to all other rights and remedies it may have at law or otherwise.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information Security Program
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The seller/servicer must implement an Information Security Program that at a minimum, meets the requirements in the Supplement for the Information Security Program requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Quality, Loan Performance Data and NPI
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae may at times share loan quality and loan performance data and other NPI with the seller/servicer in compliance with permitted purposes outlined in the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and other applicable privacy laws. The seller/servicer must use such data only for those limited permitted purposes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Feedback on New Products, Product Upgrade, or New Service Offering
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The seller/servicer may provide feedback in connection with a new product, product upgrade, or new service offering yet to be released by Fannie Mae in the marketplace. The feedback may include comments and recommendations. When the seller/servicer provides feedback, it grants Fannie Mae an unlimited, worldwide, perpetual, and irrevocable license under the seller/servicer's intellectual property rights, without duty to account, to disclose, incorporate, practice, deploy, or adapt such feedback.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Specific Transactions or Dealings
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For specific transactions or dealings, the seller/servicer and Fannie Mae may enter into a separate written confidentiality agreement. This separate agreement will control in case of conflict with the provisions of this topic. In addition, the seller/servicer and Fannie Mae may also agree in a separate written agreement that the confidentiality obligations set forth in this topic will apply to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae Obligations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will not disclose confidential information received from a seller/servicer in furtherance of this Guide to a third party, except as required or permitted by law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exclusions from Confidential Information
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The obligations in this topic do not apply to information that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: is or becomes public through no fault of the seller/servicer,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: was previously known or is disclosed to the seller/servicer free of any obligation to keep it confidential, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: is independently developed by the seller/servicer without reference or access to the Confidential Information.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: A3-4-02, Data Quality and Integrity (08/07/2018)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information about data quality and integrity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Data Integrity and Quality
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Data Integrity and Quality
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must supply Fannie Mae with complete and accurate data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All data entered into DU must be verifiable. The lender must have adequate procedures in place to validate the integrity of specific data for each underwriting recommendation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender must provide key information - called loan delivery data - about all of the mortgages that it delivers to Fannie Mae. Some of these data elements are not required to qualify a borrower or underwrite a loan but are still required to be delivered to Fannie Mae. The lender remains responsible for representations and warranties for the life of the loan related to misstatements, misrepresentations, omissions, and data inaccuracies subject to the limitations set forth in A2-2-07, Life-of-Loan Representations and Warranties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae may require the lender to correct data as needed.

elements.text: A3-4-03, Preventing, Detecting, and Reporting Mortgage Fraud (12/10/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on preventing, detecting, and reporting mortgage fraud, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Seller/Servicer Fraud Prevention Measures
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Tools and Resources
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae takes mortgage fraud very seriously and seeks to work with its sellers/servicers to prevent and detect mortgage fraud. There are two primary motivations for committing mortgage fraud.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fraud for house is motivated by a desire to get a marginal borrower into a house and may involve misrepresentation of information on loan applications.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fraud for profit is motivated by a desire of mortgage participants to improperly acquire mortgage loan proceeds for personal gain. Often fraud for profit schemes involve a pattern: two or more mortgage loans, multiple parties in various roles within the mortgage industry, and no true intent to repay the mortgage. Participants in fraud schemes can include borrowers, originators, appraisers, brokers, real estate agents, closing agents, builders, lenders, and title companies.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: There are a variety of types of mortgage fraud including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: undisclosed liabilities,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: misrepresentation of income or employment,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: misrepresentation of credit,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: identity theft and/or Social Security number discrepancy,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: misrepresentation of assets,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: misrepresentation of occupancy,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: misrepresentation of property value,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: property flips based on inflated appraisals or other false characteristics,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: misrepresentation of the subject property characteristics or comparables,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: sale of fraudulent loans or double selling of loans,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: mishandling of escrow funds or custodial accounts,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: diversion of sales proceeds, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: fraudulent payoff schemes.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Seller/Servicer Fraud Prevention Measures
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae assumes the information and processes on which loan decisions are based are honest, accurate, and credible, and that sellers/servicers are striving for information and process integrity at every stage in the life of a mortgage-from application through servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table contains general requirements related to fraud prevention.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The seller/servicer must establish and maintain written policies and procedures to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: have proper hiring practices in place.
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirm the individual or company does not appear on the Federal Housing Finance Agency's
elements.label: text

elements.text: Suspended Counterparty Program list before engaging the services of any contractor or vendor
elements.label: text

elements.text: or other individual involved in activities related to the origination or servicing of loans owned by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: report suspected fraud to the proper authorities and to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements above, the seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: aggressively sample loans that have a high risk for fraud as part of the quality control process,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: select appraisers in compliance with Selection of the Appraiser in B4-1.1-03, Appraiser Selection Criteria, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: modify closing instructions to prevent flips without lender consent.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of whether or not a breach of the Lender Contract occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter A3-5, Fidelity Bond and Errors and Omissions Coverage
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter describes Fannie Mae's fidelity bond and errors and omissions coverage and policy requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A3-5-01, Fidelity Bond and Errors and Omissions Coverage Provisions (07/25/2017)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on fidelity bond and errors and omissions insurance coverage provisions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fidelity Bond and Errors and Omissions Coverage Provisions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fidelity Bond and Errors and Omissions Coverage Provisions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A seller/servicer must have a blanket fidelity bond and an errors and omissions insurance policy in effect at all times in an amount sufficient to meet Fannie Mae's minimum coverage requirements described in A3-5-02, Fidelity Bond Policy Requirements and A3-5-03, Errors and Omissions Policy Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A seller/servicer that is a subsidiary of another institution may use its parent's fidelity bond and errors and omissions insurance policy as long as it is named as a joint insured under the bond or policy. However, if the parent's deductible amount exceeds the maximum deductible that Fannie Mae allows as required in A3-5-02, Fidelity Bond Policy Requirements, the seller/servicer must obtain a fidelity bond in its own name for an amount that is at least equal to the amount of the parent's deductible, with a separate deductible amount that is no higher than the maximum amount Fannie Mae allows for the seller/servicer's coverage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Fannie Mae will accept coverage underwritten by an insurer that is affiliated with Lloyd's of London.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will consider the use of captive reinsurance arrangements on a case-by-case basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each fidelity bond and errors and omissions insurance policy must include the following provisions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae is named as a 'loss payee' on drafts the insurer issues to pay for covered losses incurred by Fannie Mae;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae has the right to file a claim directly with the insurer if the seller/servicer fails to file a claim for a covered loss incurred by Fannie Mae when reasonably available;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae will be notified at least 30 days before the insurer cancels, reduces, declines to renew, or imposes a restrictive modification to the seller/servicer's coverage for any reason other than a partial or full exhaustion of the insurer's limit of liability under the policy; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a provision that the insurer will notify Fannie Mae within ten days after the insurer receives a seller/servicer's request to cancel or reduce any coverage.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The seller/servicer must provide Fannie Mae a copy of its fidelity bond or errors and omissions insurance certificate within 30 days of Fannie Mae's request. If the seller/servicer obtains an endorsement to the bond or policy or obtains additional coverage, it should also provide Fannie Mae a copy of the endorsement or a description of the additional coverage, unless the information can be summarized substantively on the insurance certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The insurance certificate should indicate:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the insurer's name,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the bond or policy number,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the named insured,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the type and amount of coverage (specifying whether the insurer's liability limits are on an aggregate loss or per mortgage basis),
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the effective date of the coverage, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the deductible amount.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The fidelity bond must insure against losses resulting from dishonest or fraudulent acts committed by:

elements.label: list_item

elements.text: persons assigned to the seller/servicer through an intervening employer or agency to perform the usual duties of an employee of the seller/servicer on a contingent or temporary basis and interns.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For corporate seller/servicers, Fannie Mae will accept coverage under the following types of fidelity bonds:

A subservicer must only maintain coverage for mortgage loans that it owns. (See Servicing Guide A2-1-07, Subservicing for additional information about subservicing arrangements).

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Errors and Omissions Coverage Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The errors and omissions policy must, at least, protect the seller/servicer against negligence, errors, and omissions in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintaining property and flood insurance that meets Fannie Mae's requirements,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026

elements.text: maintaining any required mortgage insurance or loan guaranty,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: determining whether properties are located in Special Flood Hazard Areas,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: paying real estate taxes and any special assessments, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: complying with reporting requirements of the mortgage insurer or guarantor.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Errors and Omissions Coverage and Deductible Amounts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The errors and omissions coverage must equal the amount of the seller/servicer's fidelity bond coverage. See the formula in A3-5-02, Fidelity Bond Policy Requirements to determine the amount of coverage required. However, Fannie Mae does not require errors and omissions coverage in excess of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: $10 million if the seller/servicer sells or services only single-family mortgage loans, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: $30 million if the seller/servicer sells or services single-family and multifamily mortgage loans.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note: If the seller/servicer uses a subservicing arrangement, the master servicer must maintain errors and omissions coverage at all times for the servicing of mortgage loans that it owns but that the subservicer services for that master servicer. A subservicer must only maintain coverage for mortgage loans that it owns. (See the Servicing Guide A2-1-07, Subservicing for additional information about subservicing arrangements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae accepts policies that provide for either coverage per aggregate loss or coverage per mortgage loan. If the policy provides coverage per mortgage loan:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the insurer's liability must at least equal the amount of the highest UPB for a single-family or multifamily mortgage loan. that the seller/servicer owns, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the seller/servicer must review the balances of the mortgage loans it services before each premium renewal date to determine whether the above limitation needs to be increased as the result of the origination of higher balance mortgage loans during the last coverage period.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For policies that provide coverage per mortgage loan, the maximum deductible amount for each mortgage loan cannot be more than 5% of the insurer's liability per mortgage loan.

elements.text: For policies that provide coverage per aggregate loss, the deductible is determined in accordance with the following table:

elements.text: A3-5-04, Reporting Fidelity Bond and Errors and Omissions Events (12/04/2019)

elements.text: Reporting Fidelity Bond and Errors and Omissions Events
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The seller/servicer must report to Fannie Mae within 30 days after discovery of the occurrence of a single fidelity bond or errors and omissions policy loss that is mortgage related and the amount exceeds the lesser of $250,000 or the policy's deductible, even when no claim will be filed or when Fannie Mae's interest will not be affected.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the seller/servicer must report to Fannie Mae within ten business days of receipt of a notice from the insurer regarding the intended cancellation, reduction, nonrenewal, or restrictive modification of the seller/servicer's fidelity bond or errors and omissions policy. The seller/servicer must send Fannie Mae a copy of the insurer's notice, describe in detail the reason for the insurer's action if it is not stated in the notice, and explain the efforts it has made to obtain replacement coverage or to otherwise satisfy Fannie Mae's insurance requirements. See E-1-02, List of Contacts for the email address for reporting.

elements.text: Subpart A4, Maintaining Seller/Servicer Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subpart contains the requirements to which sellers/servicers must adhere in order to maintain their eligibility to transact business with Fannie Mae. It describes the financial statements, operational reports, and Lender Record information that sellers/servicers must submit to Fannie Mae, and it addresses the types of organizational changes and events for which sellers/servicers must notify Fannie Mae in writing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter A4-1, Maintaining Seller/Servicer Eligibility: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter describes the requirements a seller/servicer must satisfy to maintain or reactivate its status as a Fannie Mae-approved seller/servicer. Reactivated sellers/servicers may again deliver loans to Fannie Mae and/or service Fannie Mae loans (and subservice loans for other Fannie Mae-approved servicers). It also includes information on the submission of the irrevocable limited power of attorney to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintaining Seller/Servicer Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To maintain eligibility, the seller/servicer must comply with the minimum requirements described in this topic. If a seller/servicer fails to maintain any of these requirements or fails to comply with its Lender Contract, such failure is a breach of the Lender Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the circumstances and qualifications that were in place for Fannie Mae's consideration at the time of the seller/servicer's approval generally must not adversely change after approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: If a seller does not meet the minimum loan sale requirement below, the seller's approval to sell loans to Fannie Mae will be terminated and access to all technology that is licensed only to approved sellers will be terminate within 60 days. If a servicer does not meet the minimum loan servicing requirement below, the servicer's approval to service loans for Fannie Mae will be terminated and access to all technology that is licensed only to approved servicers will be terminated. Once terminated as a seller or a servicer, the entity must go through the seller/servicer application process to become an approved seller or servicer again and be eligible to sell or service loans. Fannie Mae will review the application documentation and determine whether the seller or servicer meets the then current eligibility requirements. Contact the Onboarding Team (see E-1-02, List of Contacts) for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Sellers/servicers are not required to purchase or own Fannie Mae stock as a condition of eligibility (as stated in the Mortgage Selling and Servicing Contract).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Capital and Liquidity Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 90 days after the end of each calendar year, Large Non-depository sellers/servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit a plan to Fannie Mae that describes how the seller/servicer intends to manage its capital and
elements.label: text

elements.text: liquidity in a manner consistent with Fannie Mae requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The capital and liquidity plan must include the following at a minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a description of the seller/servicer's corporate governance over the capital and liquidity planning
elements.label: text

elements.text: process, such as oversight responsibilities of senior management and its board of directors, and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: discussion of the seller/servicer's risk management framework;
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a description of the processes to monitor and measure liquidity risks, such as business activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports and financial forecast and cashflow projections;
elements.label: text

elements.text: • capital and liquidity contingency funding plans, including providing for testing and reaffirmation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: such plans at least annually;
elements.label: text

elements.text: • an annual liquidity stress test, including a stress test of the value of mortgage servicing rights in an
elements.label: text

elements.text: adverse scenario as developed by the seller/servicer, or as prescribed by Fannie Mae, or both; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a requirement to provide written notice to Fannie Mae
elements.label: text

elements.text: ⚬ within five business days following any material change to or material deviation from the plan;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ⚬ within one business day of any material changes during times of stress.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The seller/servicer is authorized to run the stress test either in-house or using a third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: vendor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To submit the capital and liquidity plans to Fannie Mae, sellers/servicers must contact their Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae customer account team for further instructions.
