elements.text: B2-1.3-03, Cash-Out Refinance Transactions (12/10/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on cash-out refinance transactions, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Ownership of the Property
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Ineligible Transactions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Acceptable Uses
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Delayed Financing Exception
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Student Loan Cash-Out Refinances
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan-Level Price Adjustments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following requirements apply to cash-out refinance transactions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The transaction must be used to pay off existing mortgage loans by obtaining a new first mortgage secured by the same property, or be a new mortgage on a property that does not have a mortgage lien against it (the borrower owns the property free and clear at the time of refinance).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If an existing first mortgage is being paid off through the transaction, it must be at least 12 months old at the time of refinance, as measured by the note date of the existing loan to the note date of the new loan. This requirement does not apply
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: to any existing subordinate liens being paid off through the transaction, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: when buying out a co-owner pursuant to a legal agreement.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: At least one borrower must have been on title for at least for six months prior to the disbursement date of the new loan. See Ownership of the Property below for exceptions.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For DU loan casefiles, if the DTI ratio exceeds 45%, six months reserves is required.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Properties that were listed for sale must have been taken off the market on or before the disbursement date of the new mortgage loan.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For the maximum allowable LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios and credit score requirements for manually underwritten cash-out refinance loans, see the Eligibility Matrix .
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: See Cash-Out Refinance Transactions in  B5-2-03, Manufactured Housing Underwriting Requirements for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership of the Property
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: At least one borrower must have been on title to the subject property for at least six months prior to the disbursement date of the new loan, unless one of the following exceptions apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: There is no waiting period if the lender documents that the borrower acquired the property through an inheritance or was legally awarded the property (divorce, separation, or dissolution of a domestic partnership).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The delayed financing requirements are met. See Delayed Financing Exception below.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the property was owned prior to closing by a limited liability corporation (LLC) that is majority-owned or controlled by the borrower(s), the time it was held by the LLC may be counted towards meeting the borrower's six-month ownership requirement. (In order to close the refinance transaction, ownership must be transferred out of the LLC and into the name of the individual borrower(s). See B2-2-01, General Borrower Eligibility Requirements for additional details.)
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the property was owned prior to closing by an inter vivos revocable trust, the time held by the trust may be counted towards meeting the borrower's six-month ownership requirement if the borrower is the primary beneficiary of the trust .
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The above ownership policy applies in addition to the requirement that an existing first mortgage being paid off through the refinance is at least 12 months old.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Ineligible Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following transaction types are not eligible as cash-out refinances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The mortgage loan is subject to a temporary interest rate buydown.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For certain transactions on properties that have a Property Assessed Clean Energy (PACE) loan, borrowers who refinance the first mortgage loan and have sufficient equity to pay off the PACE loan but choose not to do so will be ineligible for a cash-out refinance. See B5-3.4-01, Property Assessed Clean Energy Loans for additional information.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Transactions classified as HomeStyle Refresh. However, improvements are permitted.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Transactions in which a portion of the proceeds of the refinance is used to pay off the outstanding balance on an installment land contract, regardless of the date the installment land contract was executed.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The new loan amount includes the financing of real estate taxes that are more than 60 days delinquent and an escrow account is not established, unless requiring an escrow account is not permitted by applicable law or regulation. For example, if a particular state law does not allow a lender to require an escrow account under certain circumstances, the loan would be eligible for sale to Fannie Mae without an escrow account.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: See also  B2-1.3-04, Prohibited Refinancing Practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Acceptable Uses
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following are acceptable uses for cash-out refinance transactions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: paying off the UPB of the existing first mortgage (provided the existing first mortgage is at least 12 months old);
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: financing the payment of closing costs, points, and prepaid items. The borrower can include real estate taxes in the new loan amount. Delinquent real estate taxes (taxes past due by more than 60 days) can also be included in the new loan amount, but if they are, an escrow account must be established, subject to applicable law or regulation;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: paying off any outstanding subordinate mortgage liens of any age;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: taking equity out of the subject property that may be used for any purpose;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: financing a short-term refinance mortgage loan that combines a first mortgage and a non-purchasemoney subordinate mortgage into a new first mortgage.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Delayed Financing Exception
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Borrowers who purchased the subject property within the past six months (measured from the date on which the property was purchased to the disbursement date of the new mortgage loan) are eligible for a cash-out refinance if all of the following requirements are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Requirements for a Delayed Financing Exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: The original purchase transaction was an arms-length transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For this refinance transaction, the borrower(s) must meet Fannie Mae's borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements as described in B2-2-01, General Borrower Eligibility Requirements. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: borrower(s) may have initially purchased the property as one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a natural person;
elements.label: text

elements.text: • an eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: inter vivos
elements.label: text

elements.text: revocable trust, when the borrower is both the individual establishing
elements.label: text

elements.text: the trust and the beneficiary of the trust;
elements.label: text

elements.text: • an eligible land trust when the borrower is the beneficiary of the land trust; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: • an LLC or partnership in which the borrower(s) have an individual or joint ownership of 100%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The original purchase transaction is documented by a settlement statement, which confirms that
elements.label: text

elements.text: no mortgage financing was used to obtain the subject property. (A recorded trustee's deed (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar alternative) confirming the amount paid by the grantee to trustee may be substituted for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a settlement statement if a settlement statement was not provided to the purchaser at time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The preliminary title search or report must confirm that there are no existing liens on the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The sources of funds for the purchase transaction are documented (such as bank statements, personal loan documents, or a HELOC on another property).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the source of funds used to acquire the property was an unsecured loan or a loan secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: an asset other than the subject property (such as a HELOC secured by another property), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: settlement statement for the refinance transaction must reflect that all cash-out proceeds be
elements.label: text

elements.text: used to pay off or pay down, as applicable, the loan used to purchase the property. Any
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments on the balance remaining from the original loan must be included in the debt-to-
elements.label: text

elements.text: income ratio calculation for the refinance transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : Funds received as gifts and used to purchase the property may not be reimbursed with proceeds of the new mortgage loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The new loan amount can be no more than the actual documented amount of the borrower's
elements.label: text

elements.text: initial investment in purchasing the property plus the financing of closing costs, prepaid fees,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and points on the new mortgage loan (subject to the maximum LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the cash-out transaction based on the current appraised value).
elements.label: text

elements.text: All other cash-out refinance eligibility requirements are met. Cash-out pricing is applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Student Loan Cash-Out Refinances
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The student loan cash-out refinance feature allows for the payoff of student loan debt through the refinance transaction with a waiver of the cash-out refinance LLPA if all of the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for Student Loan Cash-out Refinances
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan must be underwritten in DU. DU cannot specifically identify these transactions, but will
elements.label: text

elements.text: issue a message when it appears that only subject property liens and student loans are marked
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid by closing. The message will remind lenders about certain requirements below; however,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lender must confirm the loan meets all of the requirements outside of DU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The standard cash-out refinance LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios apply per the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility Matrix
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least one student loan must be paid off with proceeds from the subject transaction with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following criteria:
elements.label: text

elements.text: • proceeds must be paid directly to the student loan servicer at closing;
elements.label: text

elements.text: • at least one borrower must be obligated on the student loan(s) being paid off, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: • the student loan must be paid in full - partial payments are not permitted.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The transaction may also be used to pay off one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: • an existing first mortgage loan (including an existing HELOC in first-lien position); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a single-closing construction-to-permanent loan to pay for construction costs to build the
elements.label: text

elements.text: home, which may include paying off an existing lot lien.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Only subordinate liens used to purchase the property may be paid off and included in the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage. Exceptions are allowed for paying off a PACE loan or other debt (secured or
elements.label: text

elements.text: unsecured) that was used solely for energy improvements (see B5-3.4-01, Property Assessed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Clean Energy Loans and B5-3.3-01, HomeStyle Refresh for Improvements on Existing Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: for additional information).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The transaction may be used to finance the payment of closing costs, points, and prepaid items.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the exception of real estate taxes that are more than 60 days delinquent, the borrower can
elements.label: text

elements.text: include real estate taxes in the new loan amount as long as an escrow account is established,
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to applicable law or regulation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Requirements for Student Loan Cash-out Refinances
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The borrower may receive cash back in an amount that does not exceed the greater of 1% of the new refinance loan amount or $2,000. The lender may also refund the borrower for the overpayment of fees and charges due to federal or state laws or regulations, or apply a principal curtailment (see B2-1.3-02, Limited Cash-Out Refinance Transactions for additional information).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise stated, all other standard cash-out refinance requirements apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Loans qualified as student loan cash-out refinances must be delivered to Fannie Mae with Special Feature Code (SFC) 003 and SFC 841.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan-Level Price Adjustments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: An LLPA applies to certain cash-out refinance transactions based on the LTV ratio and credit score. These LLPAs are in addition to any other price adjustments that are otherwise applicable to the particular transaction. See the Loan-Level Price Adjustment (LLPA) Matrix.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As noted above, the LLPA is waived for loans that meet the student loan cash-out refinance requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B2-1.3-04, Prohibited Refinancing Practices (08/04/2021)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on prohibited refinancing practices, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Solicitation for Refinancing
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Prearranged Refinancing Agreements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Agreements to Advance Borrower Payments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Conditional Tenders of Payment
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender Solicitation for Refinancing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Sellers/servicers may not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifically target Fannie Mae borrowers for offers to refinance;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: treat loans they hold in their own portfolios and those sold to another investor or Fannie Mae as separate classes of loans for purposes of promoting refinancing.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Sellers/servicers may not, as a means of making a loan eligible for repurchase from an MBS pool (for future refinancing), encourage a borrower to refrain from making loan payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prearranged Refinancing Agreements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Sellers/servicers may not deliver a loan to Fannie Mae that is in the process of being refinanced. Fannie Mae considers the delivery of a seasoned loan that is in the process of being refinanced as a form of targeting, and is therefore unacceptable, even if no agreement for future refinancing was entered into at the time of origination. The seller/servicer must have in place procedures to ensure that it does not deliver to Fannie Mae any loan that it is in the process of refinancing or acquiring from, or funding for, a third-party originator.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Similarly, a seller/servicer may not deliver a loan to Fannie Mae if the seller/servicer (or any affiliate or thirdparty originator) and the borrower have entered into an arrangement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: for special terms (such as reduced fees) for a future refinance of the loan - unless the seller/servicer obtains a negotiated contract from Fannie Mae that allows delivery of the loan in spite of its shortened prepayment expectation. If the seller/servicer believes that there might be such a refinance agreement, the seller/servicer should contact its Fannie Mae customer account team to determine whether the loan is eligible for delivery.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: to complete a refinance transaction that circumvents Fannie Mae's cash-out refinance policies and pricing. A transaction is not eligible as a limited cash-out refinance if the borrower completed a cash-out refinance transaction with a note date 30 days or less prior to the application date of a new refinance secured by the same property.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Agreements to Advance Borrower Payments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refinancing arrangements that call for the seller/servicer to advance a number of payments on the borrower's behalf and then to refinance the mortgage once the agreed-upon payments have been advanced are not permitted.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae also restricts refinancing practices that affect prepayment patterns. Fannie Mae analyzes MBS pools that have high levels of prepayments. If such analysis raises concerns about a seller/servicer's practices, Fannie Mae may review the seller/servicer's origination and refinancing activities to ensure compliance with our requirements. With respect to any mortgage loan that pays off within 120 days from the whole loan purchase date or the MBS issue date, Fannie Mae in its sole discretion may require reimbursements by the seller/servicer for any premium paid or buyup proceeds paid in connection with the purchase of the mortgage loan. (For mortgage loans repurchased by a seller/servicer, Fannie Mae may require reimbursement in its sole discretion, without regard to the 120-day limitation.) See C1-1-01, Execution Options and C3-3-02, Accessing Buyup and Buydown Ratios and Calculating Payments or Charges for specific requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conditional Tenders of Payment
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Conditional tenders of payment are not an acceptable alternative to refinancing for Fannie Mae loans, regardless of whether they relate to a loan being serviced for Fannie Mae or to a loan that is being delivered to it. Fannie Mae does not consider a refinancing to have occurred unless the mortgage debt is satisfied and the lien against the property is released. The only exceptions to this are
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiated transactions involving seasoned loans held in a seller/servicer's portfolio that have been modified since they were originated; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: transactions involving loans secured by properties in New York that are originated under the statutory provisions that permit refinanced loan to be documented by a consolidation, extension, and modification agreement.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the transaction that permits the refinance to be documented by a consolidation, extension and modification agreement is related to an eMortgage, the seller/servicer must follow the applicable procedures in the Servicing Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: The seller/servicer must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: use conditional tenders of payment as a refinancing alternative, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: honor requests it receives for conditional tenders of payment for any loan that it services for Fannie Mae.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The seller/servicer that offers conditional tenders of payment as a refinancing alternative must not deliver any refinanced loan to Fannie Mae unless it is documented by a new note and a new loan, unless it is one of the previously mentioned authorized exceptions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Fannie Mae's post-purchase underwriting performance review of a refinanced loan reveals that the conditional tender of payment procedure was used as an alternative to refinancing the loan, Fannie Mae will require the seller/servicer to repurchase the loan in question and, if multiple occurrences of this practice are identified, Fannie Mae may take other appropriate action against the seller/servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026

elements.text: B2-1.3-05, Payoff of Installment Land Contract Requirements (12/11/2024)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains requirements for the payoff of installment land contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payoff of Installment Land Contract Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Payoff of Installment Land Contract Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When the proceeds of a mortgage loan are used to pay off the outstanding balance on an installment land contract (also known as contract or bond for deed) that was executed within the 12 months preceding the date of the loan application, Fannie Mae will consider the mortgage loan to be a purchase money mortgage loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The LTV ratio for the mortgage loan must be determined by dividing the new loan amount by the lesser of the total acquisition cost (defined as the purchase price indicated in the land contract, plus any costs the purchaser incurs for rehabilitation, renovation, or energy conservation improvements) or the appraised value of the property at the time the new mortgage loan is closed. The expenditures included in the total acquisition cost must be fully documented by the borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the installment land contract was executed more than 12 months before the date of the loan application, Fannie Mae will consider the loan to be a limited cash-out refinance in accordance with B2-1.3-02, Limited CashOut Refinance Transactions. In this case, the LTV ratio for the mortgage loan must be determined by dividing the new loan amount by the appraised value of the property at the time the new mortgage loan is closed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-out refinance transactions involving installment land contracts are not eligible for delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026

elements.text: B2-1.4-01, Fixed-Rate Loans (12/14/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains general information on fixed-rate loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-Rate Loan Eligibility
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fixed-Rate Loan Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae purchases or securitizes conventional, fully amortizing, fixed-rate first mortgage loans. Conventional fixed-rate loans are not assumable as of the note date. When selling such loans to Fannie Mae, the Assumption Indicator in the Loan Delivery application must be "False" (which means not assumable).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The payments must be structured as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: level monthly installments of principal and interest (P&I),
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: due on the first day of each month, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: payment of interest in arrears.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The loan can be subject to a temporary interest rate buydown plan, provided that the subject property is secured by a principal residence or a second home. See  B2-1.4-04, Temporary Interest Rate Buydowns for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Acceptable ARM Characteristics
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following table describes standard conventional Fannie Mae ARM requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard Conventional ARM Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae does not set a minimum remaining term requirement at the time of loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The initial adjustment period in months must align with the initial fixed-rate period in years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For example, a "3-year ARM" must have an initial fixed period of 36 months, and a "5-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM" must be 60 months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each ARM plan must offer lifetime and per-adjustment interest rate change limitations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: • Lifetime interest rate change limitations apply to interest rate increases only.
elements.label: text

elements.text: • Per-adjustment interest rate change limitations apply to interest rate increases and
elements.label: text

elements.text: decreases.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage interest rates may never decrease to less than the ARM's margin, regardless of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: downward interest rate cap.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae restricts purchase or securitization of seasoned ARMs to those that are delivered
elements.label: text

elements.text: as negotiated transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs and Temporary Interest Rate Buydowns
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following table provides parameters pertaining to ARMs subject to temporary interest rate buydowns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs Subject to Interest Rate Buydowns
elements.label: text

elements.text: Must be secured by a principal residence or second home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Are only permitted with an ARM plan that has an initial interest rate period of three years or
elements.label: text

elements.text: more.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: ARMs Subject to Interest Rate Buydowns
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: ARM plans that have an initial interest rate period of three years (Plan 4926) must be secured by a one- or two-unit property and must be structured as a 2-1 buydown with a buydown period no greater than 24 months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM plans with an initial interest rate period greater than three years (Plans 4927, 4928, and 4929) can be structured as either 3-2-1 or 2-1 buydowns (or other allowable structures per B2-1.4-04, Temporary Interest Rate Buydowns).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM Plan Indexes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A Fannie Mae ARM plan must be tied to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) Index. SOFR is a broad measure of the cost of borrowing cash overnight collateralized by U. S. Treasury securities in the repurchase agreement (repo) market. Fannie Mae uses a 30-day average of the SOFR index as published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard Conventional ARM Plans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To qualify as a Fannie Mae standard conventional ARM, the ARM must have all of the characteristics specified in the Standard ARM Plan Matrix for the specific ARM plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Standard ARM Plan Matrix is available on Fannie Mae's website and is incorporated by reference into this Guide .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Initial Note Rate Limitations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae limits the initial note rate for ARMs with initial interest rate periods of less than five years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The limitation requires comparison of the initial note rate to the fully indexed rate that is applicable at the time the loan is originated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculating the Fully Indexed Rate
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The fully indexed rate is the sum of the value of the applicable index and the mortgage margin, which is then rounded to the nearest one-eighth percent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : Unless specific product terms provide otherwise, if the index plus gross margin equals a number that is equidistant between the higher and lower one-eighth percent, Fannie Mae rounds down to the nearest one-eighth percent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The applicable index value that determines the fully indexed rate is any index value in effect during the 90 days that precede the note date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Determining ARM Acceptability
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must determine whether an ARM loan is acceptable for purchase by Fannie Mae by subtracting the initial note rate of the loan from the fully indexed rate in effect when the loan originated. For 3- and 5-year ARMs, the difference must not exceed 3%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Margin
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The mortgage margin is the 'spread' that is added to the index value to develop the interest accrual rate for the mortgage. The maximum mortgage margin may be no more than 300 basis points.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When lenders offer a deeply discounted 'teaser' rate for the mortgage, the margin is generally not used in determining the initial interest rate, but will be used to determine the interest rate for all future interest rate changes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Accrual Rate Calculation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: ARM instruments provide for each new interest accrual rate to be calculated by adding the mortgage margin to the most recent index figure available 45 days before the interest change date. Fannie Mae uniform instruments for all standard ARM plans provide for rounding to the nearest one-eighth.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : If a mortgage instrument provides otherwise, lenders must check with their Fannie Mae customer account team as there may be pooling and/or disclosure impact.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest rate calculations are subject to the applicable per-adjustment and lifetime interest rate change limitations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs and MBS Pools
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: MBS pools cannot contain ARMs with provisions that allow or require the lender or servicer to change the minimum or maximum interest rate or the mortgage margin following an assumption, unless those provisions are waived prior to pooling such mortgage loans. Since this is not a feature contained in standard Fannie Mae ARM instruments, the lender must check with its Fannie Mae customer account team to determine acceptability of the nonstandard form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If such a unilateral waiver is legally precluded because the note provision would be beneficial to the borrower and therefore requires borrower consent to waive, Fannie Mae will require evidence of a prior, duly written and executed bilateral waiver between the lender and the related borrower before allowing the loan to be pooled.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For more information on pooling ARMs, see Chapter C3-5, Pooling Loans into ARM MBS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pooling Standard Fannie Mae ARM Plans Without Special Disclosure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To be pooled as a standard Fannie Mae ARM plan without a special disclosure, the ARM must meet all of the standard plan characteristics and must
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: have a monthly payment that is due on the first day of the month;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: have an original maturity no longer than 30 years; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: be originated on the applicable Fannie Mae standard forms, with no modifications, which cover all other pooling requirements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: See the Standard ARM Plan Matrix for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM Disclosures
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must provide borrowers with disclosures in compliance with all applicable laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disclosures Regarding Availability of Index Values
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: In addition to any disclosures required by applicable law, lenders must inform borrowers that the movement in the index on which the mortgage interest rate is based can be monitored and where the value for the index can be obtained. A number of periodicals publish current index values. Lenders may refer borrowers to any of the periodicals.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders should advise borrowers that an alternative published index will be selected (consistent with the provisions of the mortgage note) should the original index for a specific ARM plan no longer be available or published. This is commonly referred to as the 'fallback' language in the note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae relies on the following 'official' source for the index used for Fannie Mae ARM plans:
elements.label: text

elements.text: A 30-day average of the SOFR index as published daily by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Disclosures Regarding Below-Market Interest Rates
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must notify borrowers of current index values and mortgage margins if the borrower's initial interest rate is below-market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless the lender is already required by regulation to make a comparable disclosure, the lender must show by example what the interest rate would be if the loan had been adjusted at the time of origination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders must ensure that borrowers are aware of, and prepared for, the possibility of both an interest rate increase and a payment increase on the first interest rate adjustment date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disclosures Regarding Conversions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Disclosures regarding conversions must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Requirement: Conversion Disclosures Must Include
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The instances when the conversion option may be exercised.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The time frame within which conversion requests must be received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The time frame within which the borrower must return executed conversion documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any fees that will be charged for processing the conversion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : Fannie Mae allows a $250 fee for ARM plans that have a monthly conversion option and a $100 fee for other ARM plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the ARM plan converts to a fixed-rate loan, the mortgage is no longer assumable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any other special conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disclosures Regarding Assumption of ARMs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Although Fannie Mae ARMs are usually assumable, some plans do restrict assumability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When assumptions are restricted, the lender must advise the borrower of the exact nature of the restriction(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : Lenders must disclose to borrowers that any ARM plan that includes an option to convert to a fixed-rate mortgage cannot be assumed once the conversion option is exercised.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See the Standard ARM Plan Matrix for information about the assumability provisions of Fannie Mae's various ARM plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements Regarding Interest Rate and Monthly Payment Adjustments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following requirements apply to interest rate and monthly payment adjustments for ARM loans:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan being delivered must not be subject to any current litigation with respect to the manner in which the interest rate and/or payment adjustments were calculated or implemented.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must not be servicing other ARMs that include interest rate and payment adjustment provisions similar to those of the mortgage being sold to Fannie Mae that are the subject of current litigation related to the manner in which adjustments were made.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: ARM Payment Shock
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: ARMs that provide for low initial payments based on fixed introductory rates that expire after a short period of time and then adjust to a variable rate for the remaining term of the mortgage loan have the potential for payment shock. 'Payment shock' refers to the impact on the borrower's ability to continue making the mortgage payments once the introductory rate expires. After the rate and payment increase, the borrower is subsequently faced with a large increase in monthly PITIA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Lenders must limit the impact of any potential payment shock on an ARM with an initial fixed-rate period of five years or less by qualifying borrowers based on the qualifying rate described in B3-6-04, Qualifying Payment Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: DU Generic ARM Plans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Generic ARM plans are provided for loan casefiles underwritten through DU. These generic ARM plans are available:
elements.label: text

elements.text: as tools for underwriting with DU, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: to assist lenders in underwriting negotiated ARMs and standard ARM plans that are not specifically identified in the ARM plan field in the DO/DU user interface (such as all SOFR ARM plans).

elements.text: Note : The term in these generic plans refers to the initial fixed-rate period. Generic plan names, such as FM GENERIC, 10 YR, are only used to submit loan casefiles to DU. Lenders must identify the applicable Fannie Mae ARM plan number in closing documents and at delivery of the loan to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Generic ARM Underwriting Guidelines
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: DU applies standard Fannie Mae ARM underwriting and eligibility guidelines to the generic ARM plan equivalent based on the initial interest rate adjustment period.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: return a message stating that the lender must ensure that the loan is eligible for purchase.

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: An LLPA applies to certain ARM loans. These LLPAs are in addition to any other price adjustments that are otherwise applicable to the particular transaction. See the Loan-Level Price Adjustment (LLPA) Matrix.

elements.text: B2-1.4-03, Convertible ARMs (02/23/2016)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on convertible ARMs, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: General Information
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Converted ARMs Removed from ARM MBS Pools
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Borrower Requalification Considerations for Fixed-Rate Mortgages Converted from ARMs and Redelivered Under 'Market Rate' Post-Conversion Options
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements for Converted ARMs
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Delivery Requirements and Security Instruments for ARMs Converted to Fixed-Rate Mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgage Documents for Fixed-Rate Conversion Option
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: General Information
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae accepts delivery of fixed-rate mortgages that were converted from ARMs either by a legally executed modification agreement or under the provisions of the mortgage instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although the ARM does not have to have been originated on Fannie Mae uniform instruments or in accordance with Fannie Mae eligibility requirements for ARMs, the new fixed-rate mortgage that results from the conversion must meet Fannie Mae's general eligibility and underwriting requirements for newly originated fixed-rate mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Converted ARMs Removed from ARM MBS Pools
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes the circumstances under which a converted ARM that is removed from an ARM MBS pool as the result of its conversion to a fixed-rate mortgages may be redelivered to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the mortgage is more than 12 months old at the time of the redelivery, and the lender specified a 'market rate' post-conversion disposition option when the MBS pool was delivered to Fannie Mae, the mortgage must meet the same eligibility criteria as other converted ARMs (as discussed in 'Eligibility Requirements for Converted ARMs' later in this topic).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the lender specified a take-out post-conversion disposition option when the MBS pool was delivered to Fannie Mae, the lender does not need to requalify the borrower or verify that the mortgage satisfies Fannie Mae eligibility criteria.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Requalification Considerations for Fixed-Rate Mortgages Converted from ARMs and Redelivered Under 'Market Rate' Post-Conversion Options To qualify a borrower, lenders may use the original in-file documentation to evaluate the borrower's financial ability, as long as the borrower is able to qualify for the mortgage based on either of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The mortgage interest rate in effect following the conversion and Fannie Mae's current underwriting guidelines for a conventional fixed-rate mortgage, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The mortgage interest rate in effect for the ARM when it was originated and the underwriting guidelines Fannie Mae used for ARMs at that time.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the lender is unable to qualify a borrower under the previous options, the lender must requalify the borrower under Fannie Mae's standard guidelines, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtaining a new loan application,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: obtaining up-to-date credit reports,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: obtaining new employment and income verifications using the acceptable documentation,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: evaluating the borrower's financial ability based on
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the mortgage interest rate in effect for the converted mortgage, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae's current underwriting guidelines for a conventional fixed-rate mortgage.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements for Converted ARMs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following specific eligibility requirements apply to converted ARMs that are delivered as either whole loans or MBS pool deliveries under the 'market rate' post-conversion disposition option that were removed from an ARM MBS pool as the result of the conversion:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The ARM must have been at least 12 months old when the conversion occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The converted mortgage must meet all of the eligibility criteria specified for mortgages that are more than one year old, unless Fannie Mae has specified that those criteria do not apply. Note : The age of the mortgage is calculated from the date the ARM was originated. These specific eligibility criteria appear in B2-1.5-02, Loan Eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The mortgage loan must be current at the time of delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: : To minimize processing delays, Fannie Mae considers a mortgage current if no more
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 45 days have elapsed since the last paid installment date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The total of all interest rate increases or payment adjustments (including any combination of scheduled ARM interest rate changes and the increases scheduled under an interest rate buydown plan) that occurred after the ARM was originated must not have exceeded 2% (for the interest rate adjustment) or 15% (for the payment adjustment) if the lender qualifies the borrower on the basis of the mortgage interest rate that was in effect for the ARM when it was originated and the ARM underwriting guidelines Fannie Mae used at that time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The modified mortgage must provide for a fixed-interest rate, level monthly payments, and amortization within the term of the original mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The title insurance policy or any endorsements to it are not impaired because of the option to convert to a fixed-rate mortgage or the actual conversion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the original title policy did not include the ARM endorsements currently required, the lender must indemnify Fannie Mae (as described in A2-1-03, Indemnification for Losses) against Fannie Mae losses that arise out of future title disputes related to the years in which the mortgage was an ARM.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The original loan amount of the ARM did not exceed Fannie Mae's current maximum mortgage amount limitation at the time Fannie Mae originally securitized the mortgage in an ARM MBS pool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The greater of the original mortgage amount (at origination of the ARM, pre-conversion) or the current unpaid principal balance must be used to determine that the modified mortgage meets Fannie Mae requirements for maximum mortgage amount, LTV ratios, mortgage insurance coverage, and title insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: EXCEPTION: For the delivery of a converted ARM that Fannie Mae initially securitized in an ARM MBS pool,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if Fannie Mae's loan limits decreased between the time Fannie Mae initially securitized the ARM and the time the converted mortgage is redelivered to Fannie Mae after it is removed from the pool, the mortgage will still be acceptable to Fannie Mae even if the original mortgage balance exceeds the maximum mortgage amount that is in effect at the time of the redelivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: BACKGROUND
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This recognizes and acknowledges, respectively, the fact that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the loan satisfied Fannie Mae requirements when it was securitized, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the redelivery is a function of an administrative requirement Fannie Mae imposed for mortgage-backed security transactions, rather than the delivery of a different mortgage.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios at the time of conversion must not exceed the maximum allowable limits for fixed-rate mortgages, see the Standard ARM Plan Matrix. If the ARM had negatively amortized, the LTV ratio (and the CLTV ratio and the HCLTV ratio)
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement must be satisfied as a result of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subsequent normal amortization
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The application of funds contributed by the borrower, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: An increase in the value of the property.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note : Increase in property value must be supported by a current appraisal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Requirements and Security Instruments for ARMs Converted to FixedRate Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must identify each converted ARM that was repurchased from an MBS pool because the conversion to fixed-rate option was exercised and subsequently redelivered to Fannie Mae as a whole loan delivery of a fixedrate mortgage with SFC 036.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders must include in the delivery package a Loan Modification Agreement ( Form 3179 ) as evidence of the conversion to a fixed-rate mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : A different (but substantially equivalent) modification agreement is also acceptable, as long as it includes an enforceable due-on-sale clause.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Agreement Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders must determine whether a modification agreement has to be recorded in each
elements.label: text

elements.text: particular jurisdiction in order to preserve the lien position of the mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If recordation is required, lenders must submit the recorded instrument when it delivers the
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage for purchase or securitization.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders must obtain a title bring-down through the date of the recordation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Documents for Fixed-Rate Conversion Option
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Execution of Fannie Mae's standard riders or addenda that provide the terms for conversion to a fixed-rate mortgage or any other conversion option instrument is not required if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: a convertibility provision was included in the adjustable-rate note, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: the lender previously agreed to a conversion modification despite the fact that the loan documents did not give the borrower an option to convert. In this instance, lenders must provide a modification agreement to document the conversion and obtain a title bring-down through the date of the recordation.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: See Riders & Addenda for current standard riders or addenda.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Recent Related Announcements
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are no recently issued Announcements related to this topic.
elements.label: text

elements.label: list_item

elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Provisions for Temporary Interest Rate Buydown Plans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The table below provides the general requirements under which Fannie Mae purchases or securitizes loans subject to temporary interest rate buydown plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: General Requirements for Loans with Temporary Interest Rate Buydown Plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Temporary interest rate buydowns are allowed on fixed-rate mortgages and certain ARM plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: for principal residences or second homes provided the rate reduction does not exceed 3%,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the rate increase will not exceed 1% per year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The buydown plan must be a written agreement between the party providing the buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds and the borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: General Requirements for Loans with Temporary Interest Rate Buydown Plans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: All of the terms of the buydown plan must be disclosed to Fannie Mae, the mortgage insurer, and the property appraiser.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The mortgage instruments must reflect the permanent payment terms rather than the terms of the buydown plan. In no event may the buydown plan change the terms of the mortgage note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Buydown Funds Provided by Interested Parties to the Transaction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When the source of the buydown funds is an interested party to the property sale or purchase transaction, Fannie Mae's interested-party contribution limits apply. (See B3-4.1-02, Interested Party Contributions (IPCs).)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender-Funded Buydowns
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When the lender funds the buydown, the buydown agreement must require that the funds in the buydown account be transferred to the new servicer if the mortgage is included as part of a subsequent transfer of servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Buydown Agreements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The buydown agreement must provide that the borrower is not relieved of the obligation to make the mortgage payments required by the terms of the mortgage note if, for any reason, the buydown funds are not available.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The buydown agreement may include an option for the buydown funds to be returned to the borrower or to the lender, if it funded the buydown, if the mortgage is paid off before all of the funds have been applied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A copy of the buydown agreement must be included in the delivery documentation for the mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Qualifying the Borrower
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When underwriting loans that have a temporary interest rate buydown, the lender must qualify the borrower based on the note rate without consideration of the bought-down rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For qualifying requirements, see B3-6-04, Qualifying Payment Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Terms of the Buydown
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae does not place a limit on the total dollar amount of an interest rate buydown.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The total dollar amount of an interest rate buydown must be consistent with the terms of the buydown period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An interest rate buydown plan must provide for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: a buydown period not greater than 3 years, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: increases of not more than 1% in the portion of the interest rate paid by the borrower in each 1year interval.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: More frequent changes are permitted as long as the total annual increase does not exceed 1%.
elements.label: text
